content.question: Under what conditions and with what legislative majority threshold would a comprehensive-system reform be reversible by a subsequent government, and what is the estimated political salience of education policy in electoral choice among swing voters?
content.reduction_path: Conduct legislative scenario modeling for supermajority requirements and reversal procedures; obtain electoral behavior data linking education-policy positions to voting choice in prior Hungarian elections; interview political strategists from both reform-minded and Fidesz-affiliated parties on reversal risk assessment.
